title: US-Iran Nuclear Talks: Progress Reported; Washington Eases Oil Sanctions After Inspection Agreement
summary: Mediators reported 'progress' following a lengthy opening session of US-Iran nuclear negotiations. US Vice President JD Vance claimed Iran had agreed to allow nuclear inspections, though Iran subsequently rejected these claims. Washington eased oil sanctions on Iran as part of the diplomatic engagement. A $300 billion fund is reportedly at the heart of any prospective deal.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of Iran nuclear diplomacy, JCPOA background, and the geopolitical implications of sanctions relief for global oil markets and India's energy security.
key_facts: US eased oil sanctions on Iran amid nuclear talks
JD Vance claimed Iran agreed to nuclear inspections; Iran denied this
$300 billion fund reportedly central to any US-Iran deal
Trump warned of consequences if Iran does not adhere to any deal
source: The Hindu / The Times of India / NDTV

title: North Korea Reaffirms Nuclear State Status: Kim Jong Un Statement
summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un declared that North Korea will exercise its position as a nuclear state. The statement signals continued defiance of international non-proliferation norms. This comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions on the Korean Peninsula and broader global nuclear disarmament debates.
exam_angle: Relevant to nuclear non-proliferation, NPT regime, and the geopolitics of Northeast Asia; India's nuclear doctrine and global disarmament debates are linked themes.
key_facts: Kim Jong Un declared North Korea will exercise its nuclear state position
North Korea is not a signatory to the NPT
Statement relevant to global nuclear non-proliferation discourse

title: Supreme Court Rules Right to Walk on Footpaths is a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to walk on footpaths constitutes a fundamental right of citizens. The judgment expands the interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) to include the right to safe pedestrian infrastructure. The ruling has significant implications for urban planning and civic governance obligations.
exam_angle: Directly tests Article 21 expansions by the Supreme Court; fits the pattern of SC broadening fundamental rights through progressive interpretation — a recurring UPSC theme.
key_facts: Supreme Court ruled right to walk on footpaths is a fundamental right
Grounded in Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Has implications for urban local body obligations and civic infrastructure
Follows a line of SC judgments expanding Article 21

title: Supreme Court Slams CARA for Red Tape Blocking Family Adoptions
summary: The Supreme Court of India criticised the Central Adoption Resource Authority (CARA) for excessive red tape that is blocking adoptions by families. CARA is the nodal body under the Ministry of Women and Child Development that regulates adoptions in India under the Hindu Adoptions and Maintenance Act and the Juvenile Justice Act. The Court directed CARA to streamline its procedures.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of CARA's mandate, its statutory basis under the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act 2015, and the role of the Supreme Court in executive accountability.
key_facts: CARA: Central Adoption Resource Authority, under Ministry of Women & Child Development
CARA regulates adoptions under the Juvenile Justice Act, 2015
Supreme Court criticised CARA for bureaucratic delays blocking family adoptions
SC directed streamlining of adoption procedures

title: Supreme Court Refuses Plea Against Collegium Picks for HC Judgeship
summary: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a plea by a Himachal Pradesh judicial officer challenging the Collegium's recommendations for High Court judgeships. The ruling reaffirms the primacy and non-justiciability of the Collegium system in judicial appointments. The Collegium system, established through the Second and Third Judges Cases, governs appointments to the higher judiciary.
exam_angle: Tests understanding of the Collegium system, Second Judges Case (1993) and Third Judges Case (1998), and the process of HC judge appointments.
key_facts: SC refused to entertain challenge to Collegium's HC judgeship picks
Collegium system established via Second Judges Case (1993) and Third Judges Case (1998)
SC reaffirmed non-justiciability of Collegium recommendations
HP judicial officer had filed the challenge

title: SC Stays Allahabad HC's Rs 10 Lakh Compensation Order in Illegal Detention Case
summary: The Supreme Court stayed the Allahabad High Court's order directing Rs 10 lakh compensation to a victim of illegal detention. The case involves the state's accountability for unlawful detention, touching on rights under Articles 21 and 22 of the Constitution. The SC's intervention indicates scrutiny of the quantum and process of compensation ordered by High Courts.
exam_angle: Relevant to Article 21 (right against illegal detention), Article 22 (protection against arbitrary arrest), and SC's supervisory jurisdiction over High Courts.
key_facts: SC stayed Allahabad HC order of Rs 10 lakh compensation in illegal detention case
Touches on Articles 21 and 22 — protection against arbitrary detention
Demonstrates SC's supervisory power over High Court orders

title: Ram Temple Donation Row: SIT Probe Ordered; SC Petition Seeks CBI-Led Inquiry
summary: A controversy erupted over alleged financial irregularities in donations collected for the Ayodhya Ram Temple, with opposition parties dubbing it 'Ghazni attack' on temple funds. UP Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath claimed the matter would be resolved within 15 days. A Special Investigation Team (SIT) probe has been ordered, and a separate plea before the Supreme Court seeks a CBI-led investigation into the alleged fund irregularities.
exam_angle: Tests awareness of constitutional provisions related to religious trusts, SC's jurisdiction under Article 32, and the functioning of CBI as an investigating agency.
key_facts: Alleged financial irregularities in Ayodhya Ram Temple donation fund
SIT probe ordered by UP government
SC petition filed seeking FIR and CBI-led probe
CM Yogi Adityanath promised resolution within 15 days
source: Hindustan Times / The New Indian Express

title: India Seeks Preferential Tariff Access in US Trade Deal Negotiations
summary: India's trade minister confirmed that India is seeking preferential tariff access over competitor nations in the ongoing negotiations for a bilateral trade agreement with the United States. India aims to gain tariff advantages that would improve the competitiveness of Indian exports in the US market. The talks are part of broader efforts to finalise a comprehensive trade deal amid global trade tensions.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to India-US trade relations, WTO Most Favoured Nation (MFN) principles, and India's trade policy strategy — a recurring theme in GS Paper 2 and 3.
key_facts: India seeking preferential tariff access over trade competitors in US deal
India's trade minister confirmed the negotiation stance to Reuters
Part of ongoing India-US bilateral trade agreement talks
Farm outfits and trade unions have written to PM Modi opposing FTA with the US

title: Switzerland Fast-Tracks Implementation of Trade Pact with India
summary: Switzerland has fast-tracked the implementation of the India-European Free Trade Association (EFTA) trade pact. The India-EFTA Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement (TEPA) was signed in March 2024, with EFTA committing to $100 billion investment in India over 15 years. Switzerland's expedited implementation marks a significant development in India's growing trade ties with Europe.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of the India-EFTA TEPA signed in 2024 — one of the largest FTAs India has signed, and the first to include an investment commitment clause.
key_facts: Switzerland fast-tracked India-EFTA TEPA implementation
India-EFTA TEPA signed in March 2024
EFTA: European Free Trade Association (Switzerland, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein)
EFTA committed $100 billion investment in India over 15 years under TEPA

title: RBI Bulletin: Global Economic Landscape Remains Fragile
summary: The Reserve Bank of India's latest bulletin warned that the global economic landscape remains fragile. The bulletin highlighted risks from geopolitical tensions, trade uncertainty, and financial market volatility. The RBI bulletin is a monthly publication that provides the central bank's assessment of domestic and global economic conditions.
exam_angle: RBI Bulletin is a frequently cited document in UPSC; tests knowledge of RBI's role as a macroeconomic assessor and current global economic risks.
key_facts: RBI Bulletin flagged fragility in the global economic landscape
Risks cited: geopolitical tensions, trade uncertainty, financial market volatility
RBI Bulletin is published monthly by the Reserve Bank of India
Rupee fell 30 paise to 94.63 against the dollar amid Middle East uncertainty

title: India Leads Global Ship Recycling with 35.4% Market Share
summary: India has emerged as the world leader in ship recycling, capturing a 35.4% share of global ship recycling activity. The Alang ship recycling yard in Gujarat is the largest in the world and is central to India's dominance in this sector. Ship recycling is regulated internationally under the Hong Kong International Convention for the Safe and Environmentally Sound Recycling of Ships.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of India's maritime economy, Alang shipyard (Gujarat), and international environmental conventions governing ship recycling.
key_facts: India holds 35.4% global share in ship recycling
Alang, Gujarat: world's largest ship recycling yard
Regulated under the Hong Kong Convention for Safe Ship Recycling
India leads the world in this sector ahead of Bangladesh and Pakistan

title: Argentina Eyes Larger Role in India's Energy Security; Bilateral Trade Crosses $5 Billion
summary: Argentina has expressed interest in playing a bigger role in India's energy security as bilateral trade between the two countries crossed $5 billion. Argentina is a significant producer of shale oil and gas (Vaca Muerta formation) and lithium, both of which are strategically important for India. The development is significant for India's diversification of energy and critical mineral sources.
exam_angle: Tests India's energy security strategy, critical minerals diplomacy, and bilateral trade relations with Latin America — increasingly relevant for GS Paper 2 and 3.
key_facts: India-Argentina bilateral trade crossed $5 billion
Argentina seeks bigger role in India's energy security
Argentina holds major shale reserves (Vaca Muerta) and lithium deposits
Lithium is a critical mineral for EV batteries and India's clean energy transition

title: Tata Electronics Suffers Major Cyber Breach; Apple and Tesla Trade Secrets Allegedly Exposed
summary: Tata Electronics, a major contract manufacturer for Apple and Tesla, was hit by a significant cyber breach. Hackers claimed to have exposed trade secrets of Apple and Tesla through the breach of Tata Electronics' systems. The incident highlights the cybersecurity vulnerabilities in global electronics supply chains and raises concerns about data protection in India's growing electronics manufacturing sector.
exam_angle: Tests awareness of India's cybersecurity framework, the Information Technology Act, and India's role in global electronics manufacturing supply chains.
key_facts: Tata Electronics is a contract manufacturer for Apple and Tesla
Hackers claimed to expose Apple and Tesla trade secrets via Tata Electronics breach
Highlights cybersecurity risks in India's electronics manufacturing sector
India's IT Act and CERT-In govern cybersecurity incident response

title: Meta Appoints CRED Founder Kunal Shah as WhatsApp Global CEO; Invests $900 Million in CRED
summary: Meta announced the appointment of Kunal Shah, founder of Indian fintech startup CRED, as the new global CEO of WhatsApp, replacing Will Cathcart. Simultaneously, Meta announced a $900 million investment in CRED. Kunal Shah becomes the first Indian founder to lead WhatsApp, the world's largest messaging platform with over 2 billion users globally.
exam_angle: Person in news — Kunal Shah; tests awareness of Indian startup ecosystem, foreign investment in Indian tech, and Meta's strategic decisions relevant to the digital economy.
key_facts: Kunal Shah: Founder of CRED, appointed as WhatsApp global CEO
Replaces Will Cathcart as head of WhatsApp
Meta to invest $900 million in CRED
First Indian startup founder to lead a major global social media platform

title: Five Eyes Alliance Warns: AI Models Capable of Destabilising Governments Are 'Months Away'
summary: The Five Eyes intelligence alliance (USA, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand) issued a rare joint statement warning that AI models capable of taking down governments and businesses could be ready within months, not years. The statement underscores urgent concerns about AI preparedness and governance. India's AI sovereignty challenge was separately highlighted, with analysts warning of dependence on US AI frontier models.
exam_angle: Tests awareness of Five Eyes alliance, AI governance debates, and India's digital sovereignty concerns — relevant to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) and GS Paper 2 (IR/Internal Security).
key_facts: Five Eyes: Intelligence alliance of USA, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand
Rare joint statement warning advanced AI could destabilise governments within months
India flagged as potentially dependent on US AI models (Bernstein report)
India's AI sovereignty and need for domestic frontier AI models highlighted

title: ICC Announces Landmark Maternity Policy: Motherhood and Cricket Can Coexist
summary: The International Cricket Council (ICC) announced a landmark policy affirming that motherhood and professional cricket can coexist, providing support and protections for women cricketers who are mothers. The policy addresses concerns around maternity leave, return-to-play protocols, and childcare support at international tournaments. It is seen as a major step for gender equity in global cricket governance.
exam_angle: Tests awareness of ICC governance and gender equity policies in sports — relevant to questions on women's empowerment, sports governance, and international organisations.
key_facts: ICC announced a landmark maternity policy for women cricketers
Policy covers maternity leave, return-to-play protocols, and childcare at tournaments
Described as a milestone for gender equity in cricket
ICC: International Cricket Council, headquarters in Dubai

title: CBSE Frames New Class 12 Assessment Policy for Students in Gulf Countries
summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) informed the Supreme Court that it has framed a new Class 12 assessment policy specifically for students based in Gulf countries. The policy was developed to address the unique circumstances of Indian students studying abroad under CBSE-affiliated schools. The Supreme Court was apprised of the development in an ongoing case.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of CBSE's jurisdiction and policies for overseas affiliated schools, and the role of the Supreme Court in education policy matters.
key_facts: CBSE framed new Class 12 assessment policy for students in Gulf countries
Policy informed to the Supreme Court in an ongoing case
CBSE operates under the Ministry of Education
CBSE has affiliated schools in several Gulf countries serving the Indian diaspora

title: Israeli Defence Delegation Visits India to Deepen Strategic Partnership
summary: An Israeli defence delegation visited India with the aim of deepening the strategic and defence partnership between the two countries. India and Israel have robust defence ties, with Israel being one of India's top defence suppliers, particularly in UAVs, missile systems, and surveillance technology. The visit comes amid ongoing West Asia tensions.
exam_angle: Tests India-Israel bilateral defence relations, India's defence imports, and the geopolitical context of Israel as a major arms supplier to India.
key_facts: Israeli defence delegation visited India to deepen strategic partnership
Israel is among India's top three defence suppliers
Key India-Israel defence areas: UAVs (Heron), missile systems, surveillance technology
Visit coincides with ongoing Israel-Lebanon/Gaza conflict context

title: Mamata Banerjee Reportedly Removed as Trinamool Congress Chief; Loses Key Aides
summary: Reports emerged that Mamata Banerjee has been removed as the chief of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) party and has lost several close aides. This development, if confirmed, would mark a major political shift in Bengal's ruling party. The TMC is the ruling party in West Bengal.
exam_angle: Persons in news; tests awareness of political developments in West Bengal and TMC's internal dynamics, relevant to Indian polity and elections.
key_facts: Reports indicate Mamata Banerjee removed as Trinamool Congress (TMC) chief
TMC is the ruling party in West Bengal
Mamata Banerjee is also the Chief Minister of West Bengal
Separate report noted BJP's first Bengal Budget was presented

title: Delhi Government vs Discoms: Rs 38,000 Crore Audit Battle Continues in Court
summary: A Rs 38,000 crore dispute between the Delhi government and private electricity distribution companies (discoms) over a CAG audit order is continuing in court. The Delhi government had ordered a forensic audit of discoms, which the companies challenged. The case has implications for electricity tariff regulation and consumer interests in Delhi.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of electricity distribution regulation, CAG's role, and the Electricity Act 2003 framework governing discoms — relevant to GS Paper 2 and 3.
key_facts: Rs 38,000 crore at stake in Delhi govt vs discoms audit dispute
Delhi government ordered CAG/forensic audit of discoms
Discoms (distribution companies) challenged the audit order in court
Governed under Electricity Act, 2003 and DERC (Delhi Electricity Regulatory Commission)
